evidence_quotes.quote: misleading interfaces should be regulated more intensively
this language is sometimes invaded by ambiguities that do not comply with the premise of providing clear and precise information to the consumer before the conclusion of such a sales contract.

paragraphs.text: Student of the Jean Monnet Modules project “DERECHO”: ‘Defense of European Rights and Excellence for Consumers HHarmonisation’, No 101239437. Project financed by the European Union. Jean Monnet Actions in the field of Higher Education: Modules. Call for tenders ERASMUS-JMO-2025-HEI-TCH-RSCH. I believe that the EU needs to take action in relation to online purchases made by consumers to ensure consumer protection, especially I believe that misleading interfaces should be regulated more intensively, as they create a feeling among consumers that their refusal to purchase will be detrimental to them. Those pages use the persistence and the creation of the false impression that the consumer has no choice other than that normally favoured by the trader, creating this persistence of uncertainty for the consumer as to a decision that the consumer had in principle already taken. On the other hand, I believe that the EU should also regulate the language used by businesses with regard to these online sales, as this language is sometimes invaded by ambiguities that do not comply with the premise of providing clear and precise information to the consumer before the conclusion of such a sales contract.
